title: Noun 並み
titleEn: Indicates 'like' or 'as' something or someone; 'equivalent to', 'around'.
explanation: Indicates 'like' or 'as' something or someone; 'equivalent to', 'around'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The Noun 並み grammar point is used to show a comparison, often translated as 'like' or 'equivalent to'. It can also indicate approximate quantity or extent, similar to 'around'.

title: Noun + 前提で
titleEn: Expresses a condition or requirement for something to be established: 'on the assumption that', 'under the condition of'.
explanation: Expresses a condition or requirement for something to be established: 'on the assumption that', 'under the condition of'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The Noun + 前提で grammar point is used to define the premise, assumption, or requirement necessary for some event or action. It translates to 'on the assumption that', 'under the condition of', or 'with the understanding that'. The following clause is based on that premise.

title: Verbる / Noun(である) + 限り(は) (kagiri (wa))
titleEn: Expresses 'as long as', 'while this condition is true'.
structure:
Verb-dictionary form + 限り(は)
Noun(である) + 限り(は)
structureEn:
Verb-dictionary form + 限り(は)
Noun(である) + 限り(は)
explanation: Expresses 'as long as', 'while this condition is true'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: This grammar is used to express that something holds true as long as a particular condition remains in effect. When using a noun, it can appear as Noun(である) + 限り(は). With a verb, use the dictionary form + 限り(は).

title: Verb がてら
titleEn: Expresses doing something while doing another; 'while', 'on the occasion of'.
structure: Verb (dictionary or -ます stem) + がてら
structureEn: Verb (dictionary or -ます stem) + がてら
explanation: Expresses doing something while doing another; 'while', 'on the occasion of'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～がてら grammar point is used to express doing something else in the middle of or on the occasion of another activity. Often used with verbs of movement or activities that easily combine with a second action.

title: Verb こそすれ
titleEn: Often used in the pattern 'Xこそすれ、Y〜ない', meaning 'One might do X, but definitely not Y'.
structure: Verb-ますstem + こそすれ、(絶対に)～ない
structureEn: Verb-ますstem + こそすれ、(絶対に)～ない
explanation: Often used in the pattern 'Xこそすれ、Y〜ない', meaning 'One might do X, but definitely not Y'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: Verb-ますstem + こそすれ is typically used to emphasize that while someone might certainly do or be X, there's no way they would do or be Y. The sentence that follows is usually a strong negative, denying the possibility of Y.

title: Verb させられる
titleEn: Expresses 'being forced to do something' or 'being made to do something'.
explanation: Expresses 'being forced to do something' or 'being made to do something'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The させられる grammar point is used to express the passive causative form in Japanese. This form is used when you are forced or made to do something by someone or something else. It's a combination of causative and passive form, and it often implies having been made to do something against one's will.

title: Verb ざるを得ない
titleEn: 'Cannot but', 'have no choice but to', 'forced to'
structure: Verb-dictionary form + ざるを得ない (する → せざるを得ない)
structureEn: Verb-dictionary form + ざるを得ない (する → せざるを得ない)
explanation: 'Cannot but', 'have no choice but to', 'forced to' (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The Verb ざるを得ない construction is used when the subject is compelled or forced to do something out of necessity, leaving no other choice. It conveys the idea of 'cannot but...' or 'have no choice but...' in English.

title: Verb ずじまい
titleEn: Expressing regret for not having done something; not being able to; missing the chance to do it.
structure: Verb-ない form (remove ない) + ずじまい
structureEn: Verb-ない form (remove ない) + ずじまい
explanation: Expressing regret for not having done something; not being able to; missing the chance to do it. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar point ～ずじまい (~zu jimai) is used to express regret for not doing something, not being able to do it, or not getting the chance to do it. It highlights an action that ultimately never happened, despite intentions or opportunities.

title: Verb ずとも
titleEn: Means 'Even without doing (X), (Y) can still happen'.
explanation: Means 'Even without doing (X), (Y) can still happen'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ずとも grammar point is used to express that, even if something isn't done, the desired result can still be achieved or the situation remains unaffected. It can be translated as 'even without' or 'even if not'.

title: Verb ずにはおかない
titleEn: Expresses 'cannot help but...', 'will definitely...', or 'must do something'.
structure: Verb-ない form (remove ない) + ずにはおかない
structureEn: Verb-ない form (remove ない) + ずにはおかない
explanation: Expresses 'cannot help but...', 'will definitely...', or 'must do something'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ずにはおかない grammar point conveys the sentiment of an inevitable action or a strong emotional compulsion. It can mean 'I can’t let it pass without doing X' or 'I definitely will do X'.
